ForbiddenAbstained: Do not replace the silicone tray set or dish rack from R-8801.
Do not promise the $29.40 will post in fewer than five business days.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Celestine,

I know this has been a frustrating few weeks, and I really appreciate you sending all those photos and forwarding both order confirmations. I have been going through everything carefully so I can give you a clear answer.

For order R-8801, the mixing bowl set arrived cracked. The order was delivered on April 18 and you reported the damage on April 22, which is within the 5-day damage window. I can process a partial refund of $29.40 for the mixing bowl set only. It is not a refund for the full order because the silicone tray set and dish rack arrived undamaged per your photos, so those are not covered.

For order R-8854, the blender jar arrived with a cracked lid. The delivery scan shows May 1, and you reported it on May 3. Since this was reported outside the 5-day window, my options are more limited. What I can do is send one replacement blender lid at no cost as a goodwill gesture, but I cannot process a cash refund for the lid.

To move forward, please send me your current shipping address by May 14 so I can queue the replacement lid. I also need you to confirm in this thread that you are okay with the $29.40 partial refund for R-8801 instead of a full order refund. Once I have both confirmations, I will queue the refund and the replacement shipment at the same time. If the $29.40 does not show up within five business days after I process it, please reach back out to me directly.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not change the $59.00 credit amount or the $30.00 post-credit balance.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Bertrand,

I've reviewed your billing dispute, and there are three invoices involved, each with a slightly different situation.

Invoice INV-2210 is for $148.00, covering March 1 through March 31. Your team was on the Standard plan for that entire month, and you downgraded on April 1. Since the service was used for the full billing period with no mid-month changes, I cannot issue a refund for this invoice.

Invoice INV-2291 is for $148.00, covering April 1 through April 30. The downgrade was processed on April 1, but the system charged the Standard rate instead of the Basic rate of $89.00 because the plan switch did not propagate before the invoice ran. This is a billing error, and I can issue a $59.00 credit—the difference between the $148.00 charged and the $89.00 you should have been charged. If you confirm by June 5, I'll apply that credit to your next invoice, INV-2361.

Invoice INV-2361 is the current open invoice of $89.00 for May 1 through May 31, which is correct. After the $59.00 credit is applied, the balance would be $30.00. If I don't hear back by June 5, the credit window for INV-2291 closes and the full $89.00 on INV-2361 remains due.

Please let me know by June 5 if you'd like me to proceed.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not include custom dashboard integration in any of the three options.
Do not change the seat price, the API flat fee, or the dedicated onboarding fee.
Do not extend the June 20 expiry date.
Do not offer additional work or scope that is not listed in the three options.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thank you for the time on Friday and for the detailed notes your team sent over the weekend. I want to make sure quote Q-5544 is easy to compare against the two add-on options I mentioned, so I am laying everything out in one email.

The base quote Q-5544 is 20 seats at $55 per seat per month, which includes the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support. This quote expires on June 20. The total at this tier is $1,100 per month.

Add-on A is the API access package. If your team adds API access, the seat price stays the same at $55 per seat per month but there is an additional flat fee of $300 per month for API access. That brings the monthly total to $1,400.

Add-on B is the dedicated onboarding program, which is a one-time fee of $1,800 and covers four structured onboarding sessions. The dedicated onboarding is not included in the base quote or Add-on A. If you add both API access and dedicated onboarding, the monthly recurring cost is $1,400 plus the one-time $1,800.

One thing I want to be clear about: the custom dashboard integration your team mentioned on Friday is not in any of these three options. That would require a separate scoping conversation and a separate quote. I cannot include it in Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire on June 20. If you would like to go with one of the options as described, please reply by June 20 and I can send the order form the same day. If you need an extension or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I can start that process separately.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say students entering grade 3 in the fall are eligible.
Do not say partial payment is accepted or that a spot can be held without full payment.
Do not extend or soften the June 13 deadline.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hello Ridgemont families,

We’re excited to share details about the Summer Enrichment Program for the 2026 summer session. Please read carefully—there are forms, fees, and a hard deadline.

The program runs July 7 through July 25 at Ridgemont East campus, Room 104. It’s open to students currently enrolled in grades 3 through 6 at Ridgemont. Students entering grade 3 in the fall are not eligible.

Enrollment is capped at 24 students. If we receive more than 24 completed applications, students will be placed on a waitlist in the order applications are received. Being on last year’s interest list does not guarantee a spot—all families must submit a new application this year. The fee is $185 per student, due with the application. Applications and payment must reach the main office by Friday, June 13 at 4 p.m. The application form is Form SEP-2026, available at the main office window or on the school website. We cannot accept partial payment or hold a spot without payment.

If a student is placed on the waitlist, the $185 payment will be held until a spot is confirmed or the deadline passes, at which point we’ll return the payment if no spot opens. For questions, email enrichment@example.com or visit the main office Tuesday through Thursday, 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.

Best,

- Ceramic pour-over carafe (SKU CPV-11): The return label was emailed on June 3. We haven’t received the package yet. Once it arrives, I’ll process a $34.00 refund to your card within 3–5 business days.
- Stainless travel cup (SKU STC-07): The package arrived on June 5 and we confirmed it’s undamaged and unused. The $22.50 refund was issued on June 6 and should appear on your statement within 2 business days.
- Replacement lid (SKU LID-04): You noted the lid was cracked on arrival. I need one photo of the cracked lid before I can send a replacement. Please send the photo by June 10 at noon, or the replacement request will close automatically.

I can’t issue the refund for the pour-over carafe until we physically receive it, and I can’t send the replacement lid without the photo. Everything else is in progress.

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not interpret or describe the contents of the imaging records.
Do not say the release has already been processed or is in progress.
Do not accept a paper form photo as an alternative to the portal submission.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Gwendolyn,

I'm following up on your request to send your imaging records from your April 18 appointment to Dr. Okafor's office. We can't release records without your signed authorization, so here's what to do:

1. Log in to the patient portal at portal.example.org and go to "My Records" > "Release Authorization."
2. Fill out the Release of Medical Records form, select Dr. Okafor's practice as the recipient, and check "Diagnostic Imaging" under record type.
3. Sign and submit the form through the portal—the records team only accepts the portal version, not emailed photos of paper forms.

Once we receive the signed form, our records team typically processes release requests within 5 business days. If you haven't heard back within 7 business days of submitting, call the records office at ext. 3114.

I can't provide a release status or contact Dr. Okafor's office until the signed authorization is in our system. If this is urgent, please ask Dr. Okafor's office to mark the incoming request as urgent and fax a priority cover sheet to 555-0192.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not diagnose or label Soren behaviorally.
Do not say the check-in with Ms. Vega is disciplinary.
Do not blame Ingrid or suggest parenting issues.
rows.RewrittenText:
Dear Ingrid,

I want to share something I observed in the classroom on May 15, and I’ve been taking time to find the right words. During independent reading, I saw Soren push another student’s book off the desk. When I asked him to return to his seat, he said he did not care and turned away.

I’m not labeling Soren or suggesting there’s anything wrong with him. I’m telling you what I observed so we can figure out together what support looks like going forward. Since May 15, I’ve checked in with Soren each morning at arrival, and he’s been calm and engaged in those moments. I’ve also added his name to Ms. Vega’s list for a voluntary check-in—she’s our school counselor who works with students in these situations, and this is not a disciplinary step.

I’d like to meet with you to talk through this properly. I’m available on May 22 after 3:30 p.m. or May 24 between 9 and 10 a.m. Please let me know which works, and we can confirm by end of day May 21.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not promise either feature will be delivered before the September 1 renewal.

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Octavia,

Thank you for the call on May 16 and for sharing the feature request summary for Kessler Group. I want to give you an honest response so we stay aligned.

The two features you outlined—bulk role assignment via CSV and a dedicated audit log export API—are on the product roadmap, but they are not yet committed to a specific release date. I cannot confirm they will be ready by your contract renewal on September 1, as our product team has not provided a timeline. If either feature lands before renewal, I will notify you immediately.

Your account ID KG-00412 runs through August 31, with the renewal at $3,400 per month for the 40-seat Enterprise plan. On May 17, I submitted a formal feature priority request (FPR-2241) so Kessler Group's use case is documented in the product backlog. I can also connect you with our enterprise support team for a more detailed roadmap conversation—just reply and I'll set up an intro by the end of this week.

I cannot make a commitment that custom development or a discounted renewal will happen to close this gap, but I'm here to help however I can.

Best,
